touchdown.say: Observation: the furniture has been rearranged into a spiral. Twice.
And something here is happy to see us. DESPERATELY happy.

look.text: The beam circle, etched into the deck plate by sixty years of arrivals. Well — one arrival. Yours.

use.text: Gold static — and behind it, faintly, a station singing to its new neighbours.
The Marigold: "Line's patched and you know why it sings, Ensign. Go TELL the station — it's still singing to strangers."
The Marigold: "Line's live but the song hasn't changed. Something's still unsettled — your team may hear what you can't."
The Marigold crackles: "Song's still on every channel, Ensign."

look.text: A comm grille, polished to a shine. Everything here is polished. Sixty years is a lot of tidying time.

itemUse.text: The sniffer holds close and reports: not a fault, not a fire. Just... warmth, and a great deal of it, coming through the grille. It has no category for that and says so, twice.

look.text: One empty slot in the rack. The others hold cells fused in place by time.
A charging rack. One cell still sits loose in its cradle, green light patient.

take.text: You lift the loose cell from its cradle.
The rest are fused solid. Sixty years is also a lot of rust.

look.text: The stores cabinet, open and orderly. Mote alphabetised the spares. Of course it did.
The drone idles by the cabinet, humming the lock code to itself.
The stores cabinet. The lock blinks a pattern — a drone-code, not a key.

use.text: The drone chirps the lock code; the cabinet sighs open. Inside: one mint comm coupler.
Nothing else in there but alphabetised washers.
Sealed tight. The lock wants a drone-code, and the only drone is... napping.

itemUse.text: The sniffer reads the lock: drone-code handshake, and nothing you can hum. You'll need the actual drone awake.
The cabinet doesn't want power, it wants a passphrase. Pocket the cell — the drone's the key here.

look.text: Dormant, not dead. Low-power hibernation — it needs a cell, not a prod.
A service drone, dust-caked, curled on the deck like a cat. Best not to prod it.

use.text: You resist the urge to prod the sleeping robot. The sniffer might know more.

itemUse.text: You slot the cell home behind its ear.
There's a socket somewhere under the dust, but slotting power into an unknown machine is how ensigns get remembered. Sniff first, slot second.
The sniffer chirps: hibernation mode, power at nil, last task — 'TIDY (RECURRING)'. It needs a cell.

look.text: Awake, upright, and hovering by the stores cabinet like it's been waiting sixty years to be useful. It has.

itemUse.text: The sniffer reads the drone: power nominal, task queue — TIDY, mood — also TIDY. A machine at peace with its one idea.

exits.name: beacon shaft
exits.look: The stairwell up the beacon shaft — a long climb by the sound of it.

effects.text: The door rolls aside; the song spills down the shaft to meet you.
